verses.latin_vulgate: Sicut divisiones aquarum, ita cor regis in manu Domini :  quocumque voluerit, inclinabit illud.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: פַּלְגֵי־מַ֣יִם לֶב־מֶ֭לֶךְ בְּיַד־יְהֹוָ֑ה עַֽל־כׇּל־אֲשֶׁ֖ר יַחְפֹּ֣ץ יַטֶּֽנּוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: As the divisions of waters, so the heart of the king is in the hand of the Lord: whithersoever he will, he shall turn it.
verses.finnish_biblia: Kuninkaan sydän on Herran kädessä, niinkuin vesiojat, ja hän taittaa sen kuhunka tahtoo.
verses.king_james: The king’s heart is in the hand of the Lord, as the rivers of water: he turneth it whithersoever he will.

verses.latin_vulgate: Omnis via viri recta sibi videtur :  appendit autem corda Dominus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כׇּֽל־דֶּרֶךְ־אִ֭ישׁ יָשָׁ֣ר בְּעֵינָ֑יו וְתֹכֵ֖ן לִבּ֣וֹת יְהֹוָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: Every way of a man seemeth right to himself: but the Lord weigheth the hearts.
verses.finnish_biblia: Jokainen luulee tiensä oikiaksi, vaan Herra koettelee sydämet.
verses.king_james: Every way of a man is right in his own eyes: but the Lord pondereth the hearts.

verses.latin_vulgate: Facere misericordiam et judicium  magis placet Domino quam victimæ.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עֲ֭שֹׂה צְדָקָ֣ה וּמִשְׁפָּ֑ט נִבְחָ֖ר לַיהֹוָ֣ה מִזָּֽבַח׃
verses.douay_rheims: To do mercy and judgment, pleaseth the Lord more than victims.
verses.finnish_biblia: Hyvin ja oikein tehdä, on Herralle otollisempi kuin uhri.
verses.king_james: To do justice and judgment is more acceptable to the Lord than sacrifice.

verses.latin_vulgate: Exaltatio oculorum est dilatatio cordis ;  lucerna impiorum peccatum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: רוּם־עֵ֭ינַיִם וּרְחַב־לֵ֑ב נִ֖ר רְשָׁעִ֣ים חַטָּֽאת׃
verses.douay_rheims: Haughtiness of the eyes is the enlarging of the heart: the lamp of the wicked is sin.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ylpiät silmät ja paisunut sydän, ja jumalattomain kynttilä on synti.
verses.king_james: An high look, and a proud heart, and the plowing of the wicked, is sin.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cogitationes robusti semper in abundantia ;  omnis autem piger semper in egestate est.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מַחְשְׁב֣וֹת חָ֭רוּץ אַךְ־לְמוֹתָ֑ר וְכׇל־אָ֝֗ץ אַךְ־לְמַחְסֽוֹר׃
verses.douay_rheims: The thoughts of the industrious always bring forth abundance: but every sluggard is always in want.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ahkeran miehen aivoitukset tuovat yltäkylläisyyden; vaan joka pikainen on, se tulee köyhäksi.
verses.king_james: The thoughts of the diligent tend only to plenteousness; but of every one that is hasty only to want.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui congregat thesauros lingua mendacii vanus et excors est,  et impingetur ad laqueos mortis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: פֹּ֣עַל אֹ֭צָרוֹת בִּלְשׁ֣וֹן שָׁ֑קֶר הֶ֥בֶל נִ֝דָּ֗ף מְבַקְשֵׁי־מָֽוֶת׃
verses.douay_rheims: He that gathereth treasures by a lying tongue, is vain and foolish, and shall stumble upon the snares of death.
verses.finnish_biblia: Joka tavaran kokoo valheella, se on kulkeva turhuus, niiden seassa, jotka kuolemaa etsivät.
verses.king_james: The getting of treasures by a lying tongue is a vanity tossed to and fro of them that seek death.

verses.latin_vulgate: Rapinæ impiorum detrahent eos,  quia noluerunt facere judicium.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שֹׁד־רְשָׁעִ֥ים יְגוֹרֵ֑ם כִּ֥י מֵ֝אֲנ֗וּ לַעֲשׂ֥וֹת מִשְׁפָּֽט׃
verses.douay_rheims: The robberies of the wicked shall be their downfall, because they would not do judgment.
verses.finnish_biblia: Jumalattomain raatelemus pitää heitä peljättämän; sillä ei he tahtoneet tehdä, mitä oikia on.
verses.king_james: The robbery of the wicked shall destroy them; because they refuse to do judgment.

verses.latin_vulgate: Perversa via viri aliena est ;  qui autem mundus est, rectum opus ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הֲפַכְפַּ֬ךְ דֶּ֣רֶךְ אִ֣ישׁ וָזָ֑ר וְ֝זַ֗ךְ יָשָׁ֥ר פׇּעֳלֽוֹ׃
verses.douay_rheims: The perverse way of a man is strange: but as for him that is pure, his work is right.
verses.finnish_biblia: Jumalattoman ihmisen tie on outo; mutta puhtaan työ on oikia.
verses.king_james: The way of man is froward and strange: but as for the pure, his work is right.

verses.latin_vulgate: Melius est sedere in angulo domatis,  quam cum muliere litigiosa, et in domo communi.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: ט֗וֹב לָשֶׁ֥בֶת עַל־פִּנַּת־גָּ֑ג מֵאֵ֥שֶׁת מִ֝דְיָנִ֗ים וּבֵ֥ית חָֽבֶר׃
verses.douay_rheims: It is better to sit in a corner of the housetop, than with a brawling woman, and in a common house.
verses.finnish_biblia: Parempi on asua katon kulmalla, kuin riitaisen vaimon kanssa yhdessä huoneessa.
verses.king_james: It is better to dwell in a corner of the housetop, than with a brawling woman in a wide house.

verses.latin_vulgate: Anima impii desiderat malum :  non miserebitur proximo suo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: נֶ֣פֶשׁ רָ֭שָׁע אִוְּתָה־רָ֑ע לֹֽא־יֻחַ֖ן בְּעֵינָ֣יו רֵעֵֽהוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: The soul of the wicked desireth evil, he will not have pity on his neighbour.
verses.finnish_biblia: Jumalattoman sielu toivottaa pahaa, ja ei suo lähimmäisellensä mitään.
verses.king_james: The soul of the wicked desireth evil: his neighbour findeth no favour in his eyes.

verses.latin_vulgate: Mulctato pestilente, sapientior erit parvulus,  et si sectetur sapientem, sumet scientiam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בַּֽעֲנׇשׁ־לֵ֭ץ יֶחְכַּם־פֶּ֑תִי וּבְהַשְׂכִּ֥יל לְ֝חָכָ֗ם יִקַּח־דָּֽעַת׃
verses.douay_rheims: When a pestilent man is punished, the little one will be wiser: and if he follow the wise, he will receive knowledge.
verses.finnish_biblia: Kuin pilkkaaja rangaistaan, niin tyhmät viisaaksi tulevat, ja kuin viisas tulee opetetuksi, niin hän ottaa opin.
verses.king_james: When the scorner is punished, the simple is made wise: and when the wise is instructed, he receiveth knowledge.

verses.latin_vulgate: Excogitat justus de domo impii,  ut detrahat impios a malo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מַשְׂכִּ֣יל צַ֭דִּיק לְבֵ֣ית רָשָׁ֑ע מְסַלֵּ֖ף רְשָׁעִ֣ים לָרָֽע׃
verses.douay_rheims: The just considereth seriously the house of the wicked, that he may withdraw the wicked from evil.
verses.finnish_biblia: vanhurskas pitää itsensä viisaasti jumalattoman huonetta vastaan; vaan jumalattomat kietovat itsensä onnettomuuteen.
verses.king_james: The righteous man wisely considereth the house of the wicked: but God overthroweth the wicked for their wickedness.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui obturat aurem suam ad clamorem pauperis,  et ipse clamabit, et non exaudietur.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֹטֵ֣ם אׇ֭זְנוֹ מִזַּעֲקַת־דָּ֑ל גַּֽם־ה֥וּא יִ֝קְרָ֗א וְלֹ֣א יֵעָנֶֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: He that stoppeth his ear against the cry of the poor, shall also cry himself, and shall not be heard.
verses.finnish_biblia: Joka tukitsee korvansa köyhän huudolta, hänen pitää myös huutaman, ja ei kuultaman.
verses.king_james: Whoso stoppeth his ears at the cry of the poor, he also shall cry himself, but shall not be heard.

verses.latin_vulgate: Munus absconditum extinguit iras,  et donum in sinu indignationem maximam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מַתָּ֣ן בַּ֭סֵּתֶר יִכְפֶּה־אָ֑ף וְשֹׁ֥חַד בַּ֝חֵ֗ק חֵמָ֥ה עַזָּֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: A secret present quencheth anger: and a gift in the bosom, the greatest wrath.
verses.finnish_biblia: Salainen lahja asettaa vihan, ja helmaan anto lepyttä suurimman kiukun.
verses.king_james: A gift in secret pacifieth anger: and a reward in the bosom strong wrath.

verses.latin_vulgate: Gaudium justo est facere judicium,  et pavor operantibus iniquitatem.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שִׂמְחָ֣ה לַ֭צַּדִּיק עֲשׂ֣וֹת מִשְׁפָּ֑ט וּ֝מְחִתָּ֗ה לְפֹ֣עֲלֵי אָֽוֶן׃
verses.douay_rheims: It is joy to the just to do judgment: and dread to them that work iniquity.
verses.finnish_biblia: Se on vanhurskaille ilo, tehdä mikä oikia on, vaan pahantekiöillä on pelko.
verses.king_james: It is joy to the just to do judgment: but destruction shall be to the workers of iniquity.

verses.latin_vulgate: Vir qui erraverit a via doctrinæ  in cœtu gigantum commorabitur.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אָדָ֗ם תּ֭וֹעֶה מִדֶּ֣רֶךְ הַשְׂכֵּ֑ל בִּקְהַ֖ל רְפָאִ֣ים יָנֽוּחַ׃
verses.douay_rheims: A man that shall wander out of the way of doctrine, shall abide in the company of the giants.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ihminen joka poikkee viisauden tieltä, pitää oleman kuolleiden joukossa.
verses.king_james: The man that wandereth out of the way of understanding shall remain in the congregation of the dead.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui diligit epulas in egestate erit ;  qui amat vinum et pinguia non ditabitur.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אִ֣ישׁ מַ֭חְסוֹר אֹהֵ֣ב שִׂמְחָ֑ה אֹהֵ֥ב יַֽיִן־וָ֝שֶׁ֗מֶן לֹ֣א יַעֲשִֽׁיר׃
verses.douay_rheims: He that loveth good cheer, shall be in want: he that loveth wine, and fat things, shall not be rich.
verses.finnish_biblia: Joka hekumaa rakastaa, se tulee köyhäksi; ja joka viinaa ja öljyä rakastaa, ei hän tule rikkaaksi.
verses.king_james: He that loveth pleasure shall be a poor man: he that loveth wine and oil shall not be rich.

verses.latin_vulgate: Pro justo datur impius,  et pro rectis iniquus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כֹּ֣פֶר לַצַּדִּ֣יק רָשָׁ֑ע וְתַ֖חַת יְשָׁרִ֣ים בּוֹגֵֽד׃
verses.douay_rheims: The wicked is delivered up for the just: and the unjust for the righteous.
verses.finnish_biblia: Jumalatoin pitää annettaman ulos vanhurskaan siaan, ja ylönkatsoja siveiden edestä.
verses.king_james: The wicked shall be a ransom for the righteous, and the transgressor for the upright.

verses.latin_vulgate: Melius est habitare in terra deserta  quam cum muliere rixosa et iracunda.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: ט֗וֹב שֶׁ֥בֶת בְּאֶֽרֶץ־מִדְבָּ֑ר מֵאֵ֖שֶׁת (מדונים) [מִדְיָנִ֣ים] וָכָֽעַס׃
verses.douay_rheims: It is better to dwell in a wilderness, than with a quarrelsome and passionate woman.
verses.finnish_biblia: Parempi on asua autiossa maassa kuin riitaisen ja vihaisen vaimon kanssa.
verses.king_james: It is better to dwell in the wilderness, than with a contentious and an angry woman.

verses.latin_vulgate: Thesaurus desiderabilis, et oleum in habitaculo justi :  et imprudens homo dissipabit illud.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אוֹצָ֤ר ׀ נֶחְמָ֣ד וָ֭שֶׁמֶן בִּנְוֵ֣ה חָכָ֑ם וּכְסִ֖יל אָדָ֣ם יְבַלְּעֶֽנּוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: There is a treasure to be desired, and oil in the dwelling of the just: and the foolish man shall spend it.
verses.finnish_biblia: Viisaan huoneessa on suloinen tavara ja öljy, vaan tyhmä ihminen sen tuhlaa.
verses.king_james: There is treasure to be desired and oil in the dwelling of the wise; but a foolish man spendeth it up.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui sequitur justitiam et misericordiam  inveniet vitam, justitiam, et gloriam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: רֹ֭דֵף צְדָקָ֣ה וָחָ֑סֶד יִמְצָ֥א חַ֝יִּ֗ים צְדָקָ֥ה וְכָבֽוֹד׃
verses.douay_rheims: He that followeth justice and mercy, shall find life, justice, and glory.
verses.finnish_biblia: Joka vanhurskautta ja hyvyyttä noudattaa, hän löytää elämän, vanhurskauden ja kunnian.
verses.king_james: He that followeth after righteousness and mercy findeth life, righteousness, and honour.

verses.latin_vulgate: Civitatem fortium ascendit sapiens,  et destruxit robur fiduciæ ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עִ֣יר גִּ֭בֹּרִים עָלָ֣ה חָכָ֑ם וַ֝יֹּ֗רֶד עֹ֣ז מִבְטֶחָֽהֿ׃
verses.douay_rheims: The wise man hath scaled the city of the strong, and hath cast down the strength of the confidence thereof.
verses.finnish_biblia: Viisas mies voittaa väkeväin kaupungin, ja kukistaa heidän uskalluksensa vahvuuden.
verses.king_james: A wise man scaleth the city of the mighty, and casteth down the strength of the confidence thereof.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui custodit os suum et linguam suam  custodit ab angustiis animam suam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שֹׁמֵ֣ר פִּ֭יו וּלְשׁוֹנ֑וֹ שֹׁמֵ֖ר מִצָּר֣וֹת נַפְשֽׁוֹ׃
verses.douay_rheims: He that keepeth his mouth and his tongue, keepeth his soul from distress.
verses.finnish_biblia: Joka suunsa ja kielensä hillitsee, hän varjelee sielunsa ahdistuksesta.
verses.king_james: Whoso keepeth his mouth and his tongue keepeth his soul from troubles.

verses.latin_vulgate: Superbus et arrogans vocatur indoctus,  qui in ira operatur superbiam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: זֵ֣ד יָ֭הִיר לֵ֣ץ שְׁמ֑וֹ ע֝וֹשֶׂ֗ה בְּעֶבְרַ֥ת זָדֽוֹן׃
verses.douay_rheims: The proud and the arrogant is called ignorant, who in anger worketh pride.
verses.finnish_biblia: Joka ynseä ja ylpiä on, hän kutsutaan pilkkaajaksi, joka vihassa ynseytensä näyttää.
verses.king_james: Proud and haughty scorner is his name, who dealeth in proud wrath.

verses.latin_vulgate: Desideria occidunt pigrum :  noluerunt enim quidquam manus ejus operari.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: תַּאֲוַ֣ת עָצֵ֣ל תְּמִיתֶ֑נּוּ כִּֽי־מֵאֲנ֖וּ יָדָ֣יו לַעֲשֽׂוֹת׃
verses.douay_rheims: Desires kill the slothful: for his hands have refused to work at all.
verses.finnish_biblia: Laiskan himo tappaa hänen, ettei hänen kätensä tahtoneet mitään tehdä:
verses.king_james: The desire of the slothful killeth him; for his hands refuse to labour.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tota die concupiscit et desiderat ;  qui autem justus est, tribuet, et non cessabit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כׇּל־הַ֭יּוֹם הִתְאַוָּ֣ה תַאֲוָ֑ה וְצַדִּ֥יק יִ֝תֵּ֗ן וְלֹ֣א יַחְשֹֽׂךְ׃
verses.douay_rheims: He longeth and desireth all the day: but he that is just, will give, and will not cease.
verses.finnish_biblia: Hän himoitsee suuresti kaiken päivän; muuta vanhurskas antaa, ja ei kiellä.
verses.king_james: He coveteth greedily all the day long: but the righteous giveth and spareth not.

verses.latin_vulgate: Hostiæ impiorum abominabiles,  quia offeruntur ex scelere.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: זֶ֣בַח רְ֭שָׁעִים תּוֹעֵבָ֑ה אַ֝֗ף כִּֽי־בְזִמָּ֥ה יְבִיאֶֽנּוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: The sacrifices of the wicked are abominable, because they are offered of wickedness.
verses.finnish_biblia: Jumalattomain uhri on kauhistus, semminkin kuin se synnissä uhrataan.
verses.king_james: The sacrifice of the wicked is abomination: how much more, when he bringeth it with a wicked mind?

verses.latin_vulgate: Testis mendax peribit ;  vir obediens loquetur victoriam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עֵד־כְּזָבִ֥ים יֹאבֵ֑ד וְאִ֥ישׁ שׁ֝וֹמֵ֗עַ לָנֶ֥צַח יְדַבֵּֽר׃
verses.douay_rheims: A lying witness shall perish: an obedient man shall speak of victory.
verses.finnish_biblia: Väärän todistajan pitää hukkuman; vaan joka tottelee, hän saa aina puhua.
verses.king_james: A false witness shall perish: but the man that heareth speaketh constantly.

verses.latin_vulgate: Vir impius procaciter obfirmat vultum suum ;  qui autem rectus est corrigit viam suam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הֵעֵ֬ז אִ֣ישׁ רָשָׁ֣ע בְּפָנָ֑יו וְ֝יָשָׁ֗ר ה֤וּא ׀ (יכין דרכיו) [יָבִ֬ין דַּרְכּֽוֹ]׃
verses.douay_rheims: The wicked man impudently hardeneth his face: but he that is righteous, correcteth his way.
verses.finnish_biblia: Jumalatoin juoksee päätähavin lävitse: mutta hurskas ojentaa tiensä.
verses.king_james: A wicked man hardeneth his face: but as for the upright, he directeth his way.

verses.latin_vulgate: Non est sapientia, non est prudentia,  non est consilium contra Dominum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֵ֣ין חׇ֭כְמָה וְאֵ֣ין תְּבוּנָ֑ה וְאֵ֥ין עֵ֝צָ֗ה לְנֶ֣גֶד יְהֹוָֽה׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: There is no wisdom, there is no prudence, there is no counsel against the Lord.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ei yksikään viisaus, eli ymmärrys, eikä neuvo, auta herraa vastaan.
verses.king_james: There is no wisdom nor understanding nor counsel against the Lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: Equus paratur ad diem belli ;  Dominus autem salutem tribuit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: ס֗וּס מ֭וּכָן לְי֣וֹם מִלְחָמָ֑ה וְ֝לַיהֹוָ֗ה הַתְּשׁוּעָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: The horse is prepared for the day of battle: but the Lord giveth safety.
verses.finnish_biblia: Hevoset valmistetaan sodan päiväksi; mutta voitto tulee herralta.
verses.king_james: The horse is prepared against the day of battle: but safety is of the Lord.
